title: Implicit casting rules of CASE WHEN ?
description:
### Discussed in https://github.com/duckdb/duckdb/discussions/14934
Originally posted by **lschneiderbauer** November 21, 2024 I am observing some unexpected type casting when using CASE WHEN in a query. When using an ENUM inside a CASE WHEN check, the result will be converted to VARCHAR which is unnecessary and wrong in my opinion: ```sql CREATE TYPE E1 AS ENUM ('v1', 'v2'); CREATE TABLE t1 (v E1); INSERT INTO t1 VALUES ('v1'); INSERT INTO t1 VALUES ('v1'); INSERT INTO t1 VALUES ('v1'); -- This returns a result only if all elements in a column are equal (namely this value), otherwise it should return NULL. DESCRIBE (SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT v) == 1 THEN FIRST(v) END AS r FROM t1); ``` Output: ``` в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”¬в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ в”Ӯ column_name в”Ҷ column_type в”Ҷ null в”Ҷ key в”Ҷ default в”Ҷ extra в”Ӯ в•һв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Әв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•Ў в”Ӯ r в”Ҷ VARCHAR в”Ҷ YES в”Ҷ в”Ҷ в”Ҷ в”Ӯ в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ ``` I expect that `FIRST(v)` is of the same type as `v` (in this case the ENUM E1), but it is converted to VARCHAR. Is that a bug or am I missing something?
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, status VARCHAR NOT NULL CHECK (status IN ('a', 'b', 'c')));
SELECT CASE WHEN COUNT(DISTINCT status) = 1 THEN MIN(status) END AS result FROM t2;
key_bug_features: Unexpected ENUM to VARCHAR conversion in CASE WHEN
